title: Pre-Retrieval, Retrieval, Post-Retrieval Optimization
concept: Organizing RAG improvements by which stage of the pipeline they target.

exercises.prompt: In the last lesson, we discussed tools in the advanced RAG toolkit. Which of these are two common layered improvements. Which two techniques help fetch and sort the most directly help find better documents were highlighted pair of techniques helps find-layer. Which two tools help improve search accuracy-boosting techniques did we learn about?

options.text: Query rewriting and vector database deletion
Hardcoding prompts and caching model outputs

exercises.explain: Hybrid search combines semantic vector search with traditional keyword search, and reranking re-orders the results to put the most relevant ones at the top.

cards.title: Where Does It Go?
cards.body: Just knowing about advanced RAG tools isn't enough; you need to know where they fit. Sorting these tools into Pre-Retrieval, Retrieval, and Post-Retrieval helps you build a clean, modular pipeline.

cards.title: The Three-Stage RAG Pipeline
cards.body: RAG optimization is organized into three pipeline stages: Pre-Retrieval (modifying the query or documents before searching), Retrieval (tuning the search and embedding process), and Post-Retrieval (refining and filtering the retrieved documents before feeding them to the LLM).

cards.title: The Library Assistant Analogy
cards.body: Think of Pre-Retrieval as prepping a query before searching a library database, Retrieval as grabbing a stack of books from the shelves, and Post-Retrieval as highlighting and summarizing the exact pages you need for your report.

cards.title: Optimizing in Action
cards.body: If you implement 'Query Expansion' (generating multiple variations of a user's question), you are doing Pre-Retrieval. If you use a 'Reranker' to re-order the top 10 search results to put the absolute best ones first, you are doing Post-Retrieval.

cards.title: Do We Need All Three?
cards.body: You don't have to optimize every single stage at the same time to see improvements. Start by identifying where your system is failing: if it retrieves bad documents, focus on Pre-Retrieval and Retrieval; if it gets overwhelmed by too much text, focus on Post-Retrieval.

cards.title: Before vs. After Retrieval
cards.body: Visualizing how data flows and changes through the three distinct stages of an advanced RAG system.

left.text: Modifying the search query or raw documents before the search runs (e.g., Query Rewriting).

right.text: Filtering, compressing, or re-ordering documents after they are retrieved (e.g., Reranking).

exercises.prompt: Under which RAG stage does 'Query Rewriting' (modifying the search query to improve search results) fall?
exercises.explain: Query Rewriting changes the user's input query before any search happens, making it a Pre-Retrieval optimization technique.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Hybrid search (combining keyword and vector search) is an optimization that takes place during the Retrieval stage.
exercises.explain: Hybrid Search combines keyword and vector search to fetch documents, which occurs during the Retrieval stage.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing stage name for the step that helps re-order retrieved documents.
exercises.sentence: Reranking is a classic Post-Retrieval optimization technique that helps prioritize the best information for the LLM.
exercises.explain: Reranking takes already-retrieved documents and sorts them by relevance, which happens in the Post-Retrieval stage before sending the text to the LLM.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following statements correctly explains how the three RAG stages connect?

options.text: Retrieval is the only stage that matters because if you search well, you don't need to rewrite queries or re-rank results at all.
A standard RAG pipeline executes Pre-Retrieval to prepare the query, Retrieval to fetch candidates from the vector database, and Post-Retrieval to clean and prioritize those candidates for the LLM.
Post-Retrieval optimization happens before we search the database, ensuring that our index is fully optimized and chunked correctly.

exercises.explain: Option B is the only generated passage that mimics an AI explaining how Pre-Retrieval, Retrieval, and Post-Retrieval work together sequentially in a pipeline.

exercises.prompt: A user enters a vague, poorly written question. You want to rewrite this question to make it clearer before searching, and then ensure that the retrieved document chunks are ordered perfectly by relevance before sending them to the LLM. Which stages and techniques do you use?

options.text: Apply Chunking in Pre-Retrieval, and Metadata Filtering in Post-Retrieval
Use Reranking in Pre-Retrieval, and Query Rewriting in Retrieval
Use Query Rewriting in Pre-Retrieval, and Reranking in Post-Retrieval
Apply Embedding tuning in Pre-Retrieval, and Chunking in Post-Retrieval

exercises.explain: Since the user's query is poorly phrased, Pre-Retrieval optimization (Query Rewriting) is the best first step to fix the question before searching. Then, Post-Retrieval optimization (Reranking) ensures the most relevant chunks are placed at the top for the LLM.
